message:
fix(core,ratelimit,store,sandbox): harden duration arithmetic and fix integer overflow risks

Systematic audit of Instant::duration_since() calls across the rate
limiting, offline store, and repair modules to use saturating_duration_since()
instead, preventing panics when monotonic clock adjustments cause a
"later" Instant to appear before an "earlier" one (observed under heavy
concurrent load with clock skew on multi-core machines).

Rate limiting (fcp-ratelimit):
- LeakyBucket::try_acquire: saturating_duration_since for leak calculation
- SmoothPacer: saturating_duration_since in try_acquire, remaining,
  wait_time, and state methods; also fix state() to return correct
  remaining=1 when within interval (was inverted: returned 1 when
  elapsed >= interval, 0 otherwise)
- SlidingWindow: saturating_duration_since in prune, wait_time, state
- FixedWindow: saturating_duration_since in all 5 window-expiry checks
- TokenBucket: saturating_duration_since in refill and time_until_token

Store (fcp-store):
- OfflineCapability::health_score: use object_count() method instead
  of direct .objects.len() for consistency with encapsulation
- AccessPatternTracker: saturating_duration_since for recency scoring
- RateLimiter::refill_locked: saturating_duration_since for token refill

Core (fcp-core):
- capability.rs duration_millis serializer: use u64::try_from with
  u64::MAX fallback instead of bare .as_millis() which returns u128
  and can silently truncate on serialize
- error.rs optional_duration_millis: same u64::try_from fix
- capability.rs tests: remove redundant comments, tighten whitespace

Sandbox (fcp-sandbox):
- wasi.rs read_leb128: use checked_shl instead of bare << to prevent
  undefined behavior on shift overflow when parsing malformed WASM

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
